requirements.summary: Fix loading states in ProductBrowse and SwipeableGallery to prevent infinite loading and ensure proper data display
acceptanceCriteria: The application loads successfully without hanging on loading states
All product data fetches complete within reasonable timeframes
Loading spinners disappear once data is available
Error boundaries handle any loading failures gracefully

file_operations.description: Fix the loading state logic by properly checking both isPending and isLoading states from useGetAllProducts. Ensure the loading condition accounts for actor initialization state. Add proper error state handling to display error messages when queries fail. Verify that the conditional rendering logic doesn't create orphaned loading states.

Review the loading state management and ensure the component properly handles empty product arrays and loading states. Add fallback behavior when images fail to load or when the products array is empty. Ensure the component doesn't attempt to render gallery controls when no products are available.

requirements.summary: Optimize useQueries hooks to prevent pending state loops and ensure proper query lifecycle management
acceptanceCriteria: React Query hooks properly handle loading, error, and success states
No infinite re-fetching or query loops occur
Query invalidation logic does not cause cascading re-renders

file_operations.description: Review all query hooks (useGetAllProducts, useGetCallerUserProfile, useIsCallerAdmin) to ensure proper enabled conditions that prevent queries from firing before the actor is ready. Add explicit checks for actor availability in queryFn functions. Verify that the retry configuration is appropriate (currently retry: false) and consistent across all queries. Check mutation onSuccess callbacks to ensure they don't trigger unnecessary query invalidations that could cause re-render loops.

requirements.summary: Verify LoadingTimeout component integration and ensure it triggers appropriately for long-running queries
acceptanceCriteria: LoadingTimeout displays after the configured timeout period if loading persists
Users are presented with a retry option when timeouts occur
Loading states resolve correctly after successful retries

file_operations.description: Review the timeout logic to ensure the timer starts correctly when isLoading becomes true and cleans up properly. Verify the retry callback is properly wired to React Query's refetch mechanism. Consider adding a maximum retry limit to prevent infinite retry loops. Ensure the timeout alert message is clear and actionable for users.

Verify LoadingTimeout is receiving the correct isLoading prop from useGetAllProducts query state and that the refetch callback is properly passed. Ensure the timeout duration (default 10 seconds) is appropriate for the expected backend response time.

requirements.summary: Ensure actor-dependent queries only execute after actor initialization completes and handle actor creation failures gracefully
acceptanceCriteria: The backend actor initializes correctly for both authenticated and anonymous users
Actor creation failures are properly caught and reported
Dependent queries only execute after the actor is ready

file_operations.description: Strengthen the enabled conditions in all query hooks to check both !!actor and !actorFetching before allowing queries to execute. Update useGetCallerUserProfile to follow the pattern shown in the authorization component usage instructions, explicitly returning isLoading: actorFetching || query.isLoading and isFetched: !!actor && query.isFetched to prevent premature query execution and modal flashing.

Review the ProfileSetup modal display logic to ensure it properly waits for both actor initialization and profile query completion before determining whether to show the setup modal. Use the updated useGetCallerUserProfile hook states (actorFetching, isFetched) to prevent the modal from flashing on initial load. Ensure the showProfileSetup condition matches the pattern: isAuthenticated && !profileLoading && isFetched && userProfile === null.
